sources.name: datacamp_rag
sources.title: Top 30 RAG Interview Questions and Answers for 2026 | DataCamp
key_points: RAG combines LLMs with retrieval systems to bring in relevant external information during text generation
Two main components: retriever (searches external sources) and generator (LLM that produces answers)
RAG reduces hallucinations by grounding answers in real data rather than relying solely on internal knowledge
Evaluation requires looking at both retrieval (precision/recall) and generation (BLEU/ROUGE) metrics
Common applications: QA/support systems, conversational agents, content generation, domain-specific research
questions: Explain the main parts of a RAG system and how they work.
What are the main benefits of using RAG instead of just relying on an LLM's internal knowledge?
What are some common applications of RAG?
What types of external knowledge sources can RAG use?
Does prompt engineering matter in RAG?
How does the retriever work in a RAG system? What are common retrieval methods?
What are the challenges of combining retrieved information with LLM generation?
What's the role of a vector database in RAG?
What are some common ways to evaluate RAG systems?
How do you handle ambiguous or incomplete queries in a RAG system?
How do you choose the right retriever for a RAG application?
Describe what a hybrid search is.
Do you need a vector database to implement RAG?
How can you ensure that the retrieved information is relevant and accurate?
What are some techniques for handling long documents or large knowledge bases in RAG?
How can you optimize the performance of a RAG system?
How does a RAG system maintain context in a multi-turn conversation?
What are the different chunking techniques and their pros and cons?
What are the trade-offs between larger vs smaller chunks?
What is late chunking and how is it different from traditional chunking?
Explain contextualization in RAG and its impact on performance.
How can you address biases in retrieved information or LLM generation?
Discuss challenges of handling dynamic or evolving knowledge bases.
What is CAG and how does it differ from traditional RAG?
What are some advanced RAG systems (Adaptive RAG, Agentic RAG, CRAG, Self-RAG)?
How can you reduce latency in a real-time RAG system?
What are some limitations or challenges of RAG systems?
How would you evaluate and improve a RAG system in production?
How do you ensure reliability and robustness of a RAG system in production?
How would you design a RAG system for a specific task?
How would you fine-tune an LLM for a RAG task?
How do you handle out-of-date information in fast-changing domains?
How do you balance retrieval relevance and diversity?
How do you ensure generated output is consistent with retrieved information?
How do you ensure data privacy and security in a RAG system?

Trajectory metrics vs. outcome metrics: Which tells you why agents fail
Outcome metrics tell you if your agent works; trajectory metrics tell you why . Production needs both perspectives.

key_points: Subagents A main agent coordinates subagents as tools. All routing passes through the main agent, wh
Distributed development : Can different teams maintain components independently?
Parallelization : Can multiple agents execute concurrently?
Multi-hop : Does the pattern support calling multiple subagents in series?
Direct user interaction : Can subagents converse directly with the user?
